tests.title: React & Modern Web Development
tests.description: Test your understanding of modern React (v18/19), JSX behavior, hooks execution order, concurrent rendering, and general browser event loop mechanics.

questions.text: In React 18+, which hook is used to mark a state update as non-blocking transition, allowing the user interface to remain responsive during heavy rendering?
questions.explanation: useTransition is a React Hook that lets you update the state without blocking the UI. It returns an isPending flag and a startTransition function. useDeferredValue does a similar job but is passed a value instead of wrapping the update function.
questions.subject: React Core

questions.text: What is the primary difference between useEffect and useLayoutEffect in React?
options: useEffect is only for server-side rendering while useLayoutEffect is for client-side rendering.
useEffect fires asynchronously AFTER the browser has completed layout and paint. useLayoutEffect fires synchronously BEFORE the browser paints the screen.
useEffect runs on every render, whereas useLayoutEffect runs only on mount.
useEffect cannot access DOM nodes, while useLayoutEffect can access DOM nodes directly.
questions.explanation: useEffect is deferred and runs asynchronously after the paint, making it suitable for API calls or subscriptions. useLayoutEffect runs synchronously immediately after DOM mutations but BEFORE the browser paints.
questions.subject: React Hooks

questions.text: Which of the following statements about the JavaScript Event Loop are correct? (Select all that apply)
options: Promises callbacks (microtasks) have higher priority than setTimeout callbacks (macrotasks).
The event loop is blocked when a synchronous heavy loop is running on the main thread.
All microtasks queue must be cleared before the event loop proceeds to the next macrotask.
Web Workers run on the same thread as the event loop and can directly access the DOM.
questions.explanation: Microtasks have higher priority than macrotasks; heavy sync work blocks the main thread; the microtask queue is drained fully before the next macrotask; Web Workers run on separate threads and cannot touch the DOM directly.
questions.subject: JavaScript Runtime

questions.text: Evaluate the output of 'typeof NaN' in JavaScript.
questions.explanation: NaN is technically a numeric data type, so typeof NaN returns "number".
questions.subject: JavaScript Basics

questions.text: What is the result of 1 + 2 + '3' + 4 in JavaScript?
questions.explanation: 1 + 2 = 3 (number). 3 + '3' coerces to string '33'. '33' + 4 = '334'.
questions.subject: JavaScript Operations

tests.title: JEE Main Physics: Kinematics & Laws of Motion
tests.description: Highly focused practice test covering Newton's Laws, Friction, Circular Motion, and Projectile equations. Perfect for engineering candidates.

questions.text: A projectile is thrown from the ground with an initial velocity of 20 m/s at an angle of 30 degrees with the horizontal. Taking g = 10 m/s², what is the total flight time of the projectile in seconds?
questions.subject: Kinematics

questions.text: A block of mass 5 kg is lying on a rough horizontal surface with coefficient of static friction μ_s = 0.4 and coefficient of kinetic friction μ_k = 0.3. A horizontal force of 15 N is applied to the block. Taking g = 10 m/s², what is the frictional force (in Newtons) acting on the block?
questions.explanation: Max static friction = 0.4×5×10 = 20N. Applied force (15N) < max static friction, so block stays still and friction exactly balances the applied force: 15N.
questions.subject: Laws of Motion

questions.text: Which of the following statements are correct regarding conservative and non-conservative forces? (Select all that apply)
options: The work done by a conservative force in a closed loop is always zero.
Frictional force and viscous force are examples of conservative forces.
A conservative force can be expressed as negative gradient of a potential energy function.
The work done by a conservative force depends only on the initial and final positions of the body.
questions.explanation: Friction and viscous force are NON-conservative (they dissipate energy); the other three statements are true of conservative forces.
questions.subject: Work, Energy & Power

questions.text: A particle is moving in a circle of radius 10m with a constant tangential speed of 10 m/s. What is the magnitude of its centripetal acceleration in m/s²?
questions.subject: Circular Motion

questions.text: A body of mass 2 kg falls freely from a height of 10 m under gravity. Taking g = 10 m/s² and neglecting air resistance, what is its kinetic energy in Joules just before striking the ground?
questions.explanation: KE gained = PE lost = m·g·h = 2×10×10 = 200 J.
questions.subject: Conservation of Energy

tests.title: NEET Biology: Human Physiology & Genetics
tests.description: Practice questions aligned with the latest NEET syllabus, testing anatomy, DNA replication mechanisms, genetics cross structures, and physiological regulation.

questions.text: Which hormone combination is primarily responsible for the secretion of milk in human mothers and its ejection during suckling?
options: Progesterone for secretion, Estrogen for ejection
Prolactin for secretion, Oxytocin for ejection
Oxytocin for secretion, Prolactin for ejection
LH for secretion, FSH for ejection
questions.explanation: Prolactin stimulates milk synthesis; Oxytocin triggers the milk ejection reflex.
questions.subject: Endocrine Regulation

questions.text: If a double-stranded DNA molecule contains 20% Adenine bases, what is the percentage of Cytosine bases present in this DNA molecule?
questions.explanation: A=T=20% each (40% total). G+C = 60%, and since G=C, Cytosine = 30%.
questions.subject: Molecular Basis of Inheritance

questions.text: Identify the correct anatomical path of oxygenated blood flow returning from the lungs in a human body.
options: Pulmonary artery -> Left atrium -> Left ventricle -> Aorta
Pulmonary vein -> Right atrium -> Right ventricle -> Pulmonary artery
Pulmonary vein -> Left atrium -> Left ventricle -> Aorta
Pulmonary artery -> Right atrium -> Left ventricle -> Superior vena cava
questions.explanation: Pulmonary veins carry oxygenated blood to the Left atrium → Left ventricle → Aorta.
questions.subject: Circulatory System

questions.text: A cross between a true-breeding red-flowered plant (RR) and a true-breeding white-flowered plant (rr) results in all pink-flowered F1 progeny. This genetic phenomenon is an example of:
questions.explanation: Incomplete dominance produces an intermediate phenotype (pink) between the two parental phenotypes.
questions.subject: Mendelian Genetics

questions.text: Which of the following cellular components are membrane-bound organelles? (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: Mitochondria and Lysosomes are membrane-bound; Ribosomes and Centrosomes are not.
questions.subject: Cell Structure and Function

tests.title: UPSC Civil Services: Indian Polity & History
tests.description: Assess your expertise on the Indian Constitution, the freedom struggle, federal structures, and legislative processes. Drafted in true UPSC standard style.

questions.text:
Consider the following statements regarding the 'Preamble' of the Indian Constitution:
1. It is based on the 'Objective Resolution' drafted and moved by Jawaharlal Nehru.
2. It has been amended only once so far, by the 42nd Constitutional Amendment Act of 1976.
3. The Preamble is a source of power to the legislature and can be enforced in a court of law.

Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanation: Statements 1 and 2 are correct. The Preamble is NOT enforceable in courts (statement 3 is false).
questions.subject: Indian Constitution

questions.text: With reference to the Indian freedom struggle, who among the following organized a non-violent volunteer corps known as 'Khudai Khidmatgars' (Servants of God), popularly called Red Shirts?
questions.explanation: Khan Abdul Ghaffar Khan ('Frontier Gandhi') founded the Khudai Khidmatgar movement.
questions.subject: Modern Indian History

questions.text: Under which article of the Indian Constitution can the President of India declare a 'Financial Emergency'?
questions.explanation: Article 360 empowers the President to proclaim a Financial Emergency.
questions.subject: Indian Polity

questions.text: Which of the following constitutional provisions are key pillars of Indian Federalism? (Select all that apply)
options: An independent judiciary to resolve disputes.
Division of powers between Union and State lists.
questions.explanation: India has SINGLE citizenship, so statement 2 is false; the rest are true pillars of federalism.
questions.subject: Federal Structure

questions.text: How many schedules are currently present in the Constitution of India?
questions.explanation: Originally 8 schedules; currently there are 12 after amendments.
questions.subject: Constitutional Schedules

tests.title: General Aptitude: Reasoning & Quantitative
tests.description: Evaluate your mental math and logical deductions with standard reasoning questions involving sequences, work rate ratios, speed calculations, and relative structures.

questions.text: A and B can complete a piece of work in 12 days and 18 days respectively. If they work together, how many days will it take them to finish the entire work?
questions.explanation: Joint rate = 1/12 + 1/18 = 5/36 per day → 36/5 = 7.2 days.
questions.subject: Work and Time

questions.text: Complete the letter series: SCD, TEF, UGH, ____, WKL
questions.explanation: First letters go S,T,U,V,W; pairs go CD,EF,GH,IJ,KL → VIJ.
questions.subject: Logical Series

questions.text: A train running at a speed of 72 km/h crosses a stationary telegraph post in 15 seconds. What is the length of the train in meters?
questions.subject: Speed & Distance

questions.text: A shopkeeper sells a product for $240, making a profit of 20%. What was the cost price of the product in dollars?
questions.subject: Profit and Loss

questions.text: In a family, there are a husband, a wife, two sons, and two daughters. All daughters are invited to a feast. Both sons went out to play. The husband is at work. Who is left at home?
questions.explanation: Daughters at the feast, sons out playing, husband at work — only the Wife remains home.
questions.subject: Logical Deduction

tests.title: Bank PO/Clerk: Quantitative Aptitude & Reasoning
tests.description: Practice set aligned with IBPS PO, IBPS Clerk, and SBI exam patterns — covering simplification, ratio, seating arrangement, and number series.

questions.text: The simple interest on a sum of ₹8,000 at a rate of 5% per annum for 3 years is:
questions.subject: Simple Interest

questions.text: Find the next number in the series: 2, 6, 12, 20, 30, ?
questions.explanation: Differences are 4,6,8,10,12 → pattern n(n+1): 30+12=42.
questions.subject: Number Series

questions.text: In a row of 40 students, Rahul is 12th from the left. What is his position from the right?
questions.explanation: Position from right = (Total - position from left) + 1 = (40-12)+1 = 29.
questions.subject: Seating Arrangement

questions.text: Which of the following statements about the RBI's monetary policy tools are correct? (Select all that apply)
options: Repo rate is the rate at which RBI lends to commercial banks.
CRR is the percentage of deposits banks must hold as cash with RBI.
SLR requires banks to hold a portion of deposits in liquid assets like gold or government securities.
Bank rate and repo rate are always identical.
questions.explanation: Repo rate, CRR, and SLR are correctly described; bank rate and repo rate are distinct tools and not always equal.
questions.subject: Banking Awareness

tests.title: SSC CGL/CHSL: General Awareness & Quant
tests.description: Covers SSC-pattern static GK, simplification, percentage problems, and English grammar basics for CGL, CHSL, and MTS aspirants.

questions.text: Who is regarded as the Father of the Indian Constitution?
questions.explanation: Dr. B.R. Ambedkar chaired the Drafting Committee of the Constituent Assembly.
questions.subject: Static GK

questions.text: A number increased by 20% becomes 180. What is the original number?
questions.explanation: Let x be the number: 1.2x = 180 → x = 150.
questions.subject: Percentage

questions.text: Choose the correctly spelled word.
questions.explanation: 'Occurred' is spelled correctly; the others should be 'Receive', 'Achieve', and 'Separate'.
questions.subject: English Grammar

questions.text: The headquarters of the United Nations is located in:
questions.explanation: The UN headquarters is in New York City, USA.
questions.subject: Static GK

tests.title: Railway RRB NTPC/Group D: General Science & Math
tests.description: RRB-pattern questions on general science, basic mathematics, and current affairs relevant to NTPC, Group D, and ALP exams.

questions.text: Which gas is most abundant in the Earth's atmosphere?
questions.explanation: Nitrogen makes up about 78% of the Earth's atmosphere.
questions.subject: General Science

questions.text: A train 150m long crosses a platform 250m long in 20 seconds. What is the speed of the train in m/s?
questions.explanation: Total distance = 150+250 = 400m. Speed = 400/20 = 20 m/s.
questions.subject: Speed & Distance

questions.text: The SI unit of electric current is:
questions.explanation: The ampere (A) is the SI unit of electric current.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.text: Which of the following are organelles found in a typical animal cell? (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: Mitochondria and Golgi apparatus are present in animal cells; chloroplast and cell wall are found in plant cells, not animal cells.
questions.subject: Biology

tests.title: State PSC / TET: Teaching Aptitude & Pedagogy
tests.description: Practice questions on child development, teaching methods, and pedagogy for State PSC, State TET, and CTET aspirants.

questions.text: According to Piaget's theory, a child in the 'Concrete Operational' stage typically falls in which age range?
questions.explanation: The Concrete Operational stage in Piaget's theory spans roughly ages 7 to 11 years.
questions.subject: Child Development

questions.text: Which of the following are formative assessment techniques? (Select all that apply)
options: Class quizzes and quick oral questions
questions.explanation: Formative assessment happens during learning (quizzes, peer feedback, observation); the final board exam is a summative assessment.
questions.subject: Assessment & Evaluation

questions.text: The Right to Education (RTE) Act, 2009 guarantees free and compulsory education for children of which age group?
questions.explanation: The RTE Act, 2009 mandates free and compulsory education for children aged 6 to 14 years.
questions.subject: Education Policy

tests.title: NDA/CDS: General Knowledge & Reasoning
tests.description: Defence exam-pattern questions on current affairs, basic reasoning, and general science for NDA and CDS aspirants.

questions.text: Who is the current Chief of Defence Staff (CDS) of India, as per your general awareness of the post's role — what is the CDS primarily responsible for?
options: Commanding all three armed forces in battle directly
Acting as the single-point military advisor to the government
Managing only the Indian Air Force
questions.explanation: The Chief of Defence Staff acts as the principal military advisor to the Government of India and heads the Department of Military Affairs.
questions.subject: Defence & Current Affairs

questions.text: If North becomes East, East becomes South, and so on (each direction rotates 90° clockwise), what was the original direction if a person is now facing South?
questions.explanation: Rotating 90° clockwise: North→East→South→West→North. Working backward from South, the original direction was West.
questions.subject: Reasoning

questions.text: Which Indian missile system is a supersonic cruise missile jointly developed with Russia?
questions.explanation: BrahMos is a supersonic cruise missile jointly developed by India and Russia.
questions.subject: Defence & Current Affairs

tests.title: IBPS PO Prelims Practice Set
tests.description: Practice quantitative aptitude, reasoning, and banking awareness questions in the pattern of the IBPS PO Preliminary exam.

questions.text: If the cost price of an item is ₹450 and it is sold at a profit of 20%, what is the selling price (in ₹)?
questions.subject: Profit & Loss

questions.text: Pointing to a photograph, a man says, 'She is the daughter of my grandfather's only son.' How is the woman related to the man?
questions.explanation: The grandfather's only son is the man's father, so the daughter is the man's sister.
questions.subject: Blood Relations

questions.text: The headquarters of the Reserve Bank of India is located in:
questions.explanation: The Reserve Bank of India is headquartered in Mumbai.
questions.subject: Banking Awareness

questions.text: A sum of ₹8,000 amounts to ₹9,800 in 3 years at simple interest. What is the rate of interest per annum (in %)?
questions.subject: Simple Interest

questions.text: In a certain code language, 'SUN' is written as 'TVO'. How is 'MOON' written in that code?
questions.explanation: Each letter is shifted forward by one place: M→N, O→P, O→P, N→O, giving NPPO.
questions.subject: Coding-Decoding

questions.text: The average of 5 consecutive odd numbers is 51. What is the largest number among them?
questions.explanation: For 5 consecutive odd numbers, the average equals the middle number, so the middle term is 51. The numbers are 47, 49, 51, 53, 55, so the largest is 55.
questions.subject: Averages

questions.text: Which committee's recommendations led to the licensing framework for India's Small Finance Banks and Payments Banks?
questions.explanation: The Nachiket Mor Committee (2013-14) on Comprehensive Financial Services recommended differentiated bank licenses, leading to the creation of Small Finance Banks and Payments Banks.
questions.subject: Banking Awareness

questions.text: What does 'NEFT' stand for in the Indian banking system?
questions.explanation: NEFT stands for National Electronic Funds Transfer, an electronic payment system for transferring funds between bank accounts in India.
questions.subject: Banking Awareness

questions.text: Which of the following statements about the Repo Rate are correct? (Select all that apply)
options: It is the rate at which RBI lends money to commercial banks
An increase in repo rate generally makes loans costlier
It is decided by the Monetary Policy Committee (MPC)
It is the rate at which banks lend money to RBI
questions.explanation: Repo rate is the rate at which RBI lends to commercial banks, set by the MPC; raising it makes borrowing costlier. The rate at which banks lend to RBI is called the reverse repo rate, not the repo rate.
questions.subject: Banking Awareness

questions.text: Complete the number series: 2, 6, 12, 20, 30, ?
questions.explanation: The differences between consecutive terms increase by 2 each time (4,6,8,10,12), so the next term is 30 + 12 = 42.
questions.subject: Number Series

tests.title: IBPS Clerk Prelims Practice Set
tests.description: Quant and reasoning practice aligned with the IBPS Clerk Preliminary exam pattern.

questions.text: The average of five consecutive even numbers is 24. What is the largest number among them?
questions.explanation: Numbers: 20,22,24,26,28 — average is 24, largest is 28.
questions.subject: Averages

questions.text: Complete the analogy: Doctor : Hospital :: Teacher : ?
questions.explanation: A doctor works in a hospital just as a teacher works in a school.
questions.subject: Analogy

questions.text: 'UPI' in digital banking stands for:
questions.explanation: UPI stands for Unified Payments Interface, enabling instant fund transfers in India.
questions.subject: Banking Awareness

tests.title: SBI PO Prelims Practice Set
tests.description: Practice set covering quantitative aptitude, reasoning, and banking awareness for SBI PO aspirants.

questions.text: A sum of ₹5,000 amounts to ₹5,800 in 2 years at simple interest. What is the rate of interest per annum (in %)?
questions.subject: Simple Interest

questions.text: In a certain code, 'BOOK' is written as 'CPPL'. How is 'PAGE' written in that code?
questions.explanation: Each letter is shifted forward by one place: P→Q, A→B, G→H, E→F, giving QBHF.
questions.subject: Coding-Decoding

questions.text: Which committee recommended the establishment of Regional Rural Banks (RRBs) in India?
questions.explanation: The Sivaraman Committee (1975) recommended setting up Regional Rural Banks.
questions.subject: Banking Awareness

tests.title: SBI Clerk Prelims Practice Set
tests.description: Foundational quant and reasoning questions in the SBI Clerk exam pattern.

questions.text: What is 15% of 340?
questions.subject: Percentage

questions.text: Which number will replace the question mark? 5, 10, 20, 40, ?
questions.explanation: Each number is double the previous one: 40x2=80.
questions.subject: Number Series

questions.text: NABARD stands for:
options: National Bank for Agriculture and Rural Development
National Board for Agricultural Research and Development
National Association for Banking and Rural Development
questions.explanation: NABARD is the National Bank for Agriculture and Rural Development.
questions.subject: Banking Awareness

tests.title: RBI Assistant Practice Set
tests.description: Practice questions covering quant, reasoning, and RBI-related banking awareness.

questions.text: Two pipes can fill a tank in 20 and 30 minutes respectively. How long will both pipes take to fill the tank together (in minutes)?
questions.explanation: Combined rate = 1/20+1/30 = 1/12, so time = 12 minutes.
questions.subject: Time & Work

questions.text: NABARD stands for:
options: National Bank for Agriculture and Rural Development
National Board for Agricultural Research and Development
National Association for Banking and Rural Development
questions.explanation: NABARD is the National Bank for Agriculture and Rural Development.
questions.subject: Banking Awareness

questions.text: If South-East becomes North, North-East becomes West, what does South become?
questions.explanation: Each direction is rotated 135° anticlockwise; South maps to North-West.
questions.subject: Direction Sense

tests.title: RBI Grade B Practice Set
tests.description: Higher-difficulty quant and banking awareness questions for RBI Grade B officer aspirants.

questions.text: NABARD stands for:
options: National Bank for Agriculture and Rural Development
National Board for Agricultural Research and Development
National Association for Banking and Rural Development
questions.explanation: NABARD is the National Bank for Agriculture and Rural Development.
questions.subject: Banking Awareness

questions.text: The perimeter of a square is 64 cm. What is its area (in sq. cm)?
questions.explanation: Side = 64/4 = 16 cm. Area = 16x16 = 256 sq cm.
questions.subject: Mensuration

questions.text: A is the brother of B. C is the mother of A. D is the father of C. How is B related to D?
questions.explanation: C is A's mother, so C is also B's mother; D is C's father, making D the grandfather of both A and B.
questions.subject: Blood Relations

tests.title: NABARD Grade A Practice Set
tests.description: Practice set on banking awareness, quant, and reasoning for NABARD Grade A aspirants.

questions.text: The headquarters of the Reserve Bank of India is located in:
questions.explanation: The Reserve Bank of India is headquartered in Mumbai.
questions.subject: Banking Awareness

questions.text: The LCM of 12 and 18 is:
questions.explanation: Multiples: 12x3=36, 18x2=36, so LCM = 36.
questions.subject: Number System

questions.text: The Battle of Plassey was fought in the year:
questions.explanation: The Battle of Plassey, fought in 1757, marked the beginning of British political control in India.
questions.subject: Modern Indian History

tests.title: LIC AAO Practice Set
tests.description: Quantitative aptitude and reasoning questions in the LIC AAO exam pattern.

questions.text: The LCM of 12 and 18 is:

questions.subject: Number System

questions.text: Find the odd one out.
questions.explanation: Potato is a vegetable while the other three are fruits.
questions.subject: Classification

questions.text: Which is the smallest state in India by area?
questions.explanation: Goa is the smallest Indian state by geographical area.
questions.subject: Indian Geography

tests.title: LIC ADO Practice Set

questions.text: Find the odd one out.

questions.text: Which is the smallest state in India by area?
questions.explanation: Goa is the smallest Indian state by geographical area.
questions.subject: Indian Geography

questions.text: The ratio of two numbers is 3:4 and their sum is 63. What is the smaller number?
questions.explanation: 3x+4x=63 → x=9, smaller number = 3x9=27.
questions.subject: Ratio & Proportion

tests.title: NICL AO Practice Set
tests.description: Practice questions on reasoning, quant, and general awareness for insurance sector exams.

questions.text: Pointing to a photograph, a man says, 'She is the daughter of my grandfather's only son.' How is the woman related to the man?
questions.explanation: The grandfather's only son is the man's father, so the daughter is the man's sister.
questions.subject: Blood Relations

questions.text: The ratio of two numbers is 3:4 and their sum is 63. What is the smaller number?
questions.explanation: 3x+4x=63 → x=9, smaller number = 3x9=27.

questions.text: Which Article of the Indian Constitution abolishes untouchability?
questions.explanation: Article 17 of the Indian Constitution abolishes untouchability in any form.
questions.subject: Indian Polity

questions.text: The ratio of two numbers is 3:4 and their sum is 63. What is the smaller number?

questions.subject: Ratio & Proportion

questions.text: In a certain code, 'BOOK' is written as 'CPPL'. How is 'PAGE' written in that code?
questions.explanation: Each letter is shifted forward by one place: P→Q, A→B, G→H, E→F, giving QBHF.
questions.subject: Coding-Decoding

questions.text: Who was the first President of independent India?
questions.explanation: Dr. Rajendra Prasad served as the first President of India from 1950 to 1962.
questions.subject: Static GK

questions.text: A train 150 m long crosses a pole in 10 seconds. What is the speed of the train in km/h?
questions.explanation: Speed = distance/time = 150/10 = 15 m/s. Converting to km/h: 15 x 18/5 = 54 km/h.
questions.subject: Time, Speed & Distance

questions.text: Who is popularly known as the 'Missile Man of India'?
questions.explanation: Dr. A.P.J. Abdul Kalam, who later became the 11th President of India, is known as the 'Missile Man of India' for his contributions to missile and space technology.
questions.subject: Static GK

questions.text: The Battle of Plassey, which laid the foundation of British rule in India, was fought in the year:
questions.explanation: The Battle of Plassey was fought in 1757 between the British East India Company under Robert Clive and Siraj-ud-Daulah, the Nawab of Bengal.
questions.subject: Modern Indian History

questions.text: Find the value of: 25% of 480 + 10% of 250
questions.subject: Percentage

questions.text: Which Indian state has the longest coastline?
questions.explanation: Gujarat has India's longest coastline among states, at roughly 1,600 km.
questions.subject: Indian Geography

questions.text: Choose the correctly spelled word:
questions.explanation: The correct spelling follows the rule 'i before e except after c' — 'Receive' is correct since it comes after 'c'.
questions.subject: English Spelling

questions.text: Which of the following are classical dance forms of India? (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: Bharatanatyam, Kathak, and Odissi are among India's recognized classical dance forms; Salsa is a Latin American social dance, not Indian classical.
questions.subject: Indian Culture

questions.explanation: The Konark Sun Temple, a UNESCO World Heritage Site, is located in Odisha.

questions.explanation: The SI unit of force is the Newton (N).

tests.title: CTET Paper 1 Practice Set
tests.description: Child development, pedagogy, English grammar, and general knowledge practice for CTET Paper 1 aspirants.

questions.text: The Sun Temple at Konark is located in which state?
questions.explanation: The Konark Sun Temple, a UNESCO World Heritage Site, is located in Odisha.
questions.subject: Static GK

questions.text: If South-East becomes North, North-East becomes West, what does South become?
questions.explanation: Each direction is rotated 135° anticlockwise; South maps to North-West.
questions.subject: Direction Sense

questions.text: Fill in the blank: She has been working here ___ 2015.
questions.explanation: 'Since' is used with a specific point in time (2015).
questions.subject: English Grammar

questions.text: According to Piaget's theory of cognitive development, a child in the 'concrete operational stage' (approx. 7–11 years) is primarily able to:
options: Think abstractly about purely hypothetical situations
Perform logical operations on concrete objects and events
Understand object permanence for the very first time
questions.explanation: Piaget's concrete operational stage is marked by logical thinking about tangible, concrete objects and events; abstract hypothetical reasoning develops later in the formal operational stage.
questions.subject: Child Development & Pedagogy

questions.text: Which of the following best reflects a 'constructivist' approach to teaching?
options: The teacher lectures and students memorize facts by rote
Students actively construct knowledge through experience, exploration, and prior understanding
Learning is measured only through standardized written tests
Students are penalized for wrong answers to enforce correct ones
questions.explanation: Constructivism, associated with Piaget and Vygotsky, holds that learners actively build knowledge through experience rather than passively receiving it from the teacher.
questions.subject: Child Development & Pedagogy

questions.text: The concept of the 'Zone of Proximal Development (ZPD)' was proposed by:
questions.explanation: Lev Vygotsky introduced the Zone of Proximal Development — the gap between what a learner can do alone and what they can do with guidance.
questions.subject: Child Development & Pedagogy

questions.text: In a class, the ratio of boys to girls is 5:4. If there are 20 boys, how many girls are there?
questions.explanation: 5x = 20 → x = 4, so the number of girls = 4x = 16.
questions.subject: Reasoning & Numerical Ability

questions.text: Choose the correct passive voice of: 'She is writing a letter.'
options: A letter is written by her
A letter is being written by her
A letter was written by her
A letter has been written by her
questions.explanation: The present continuous tense 'is writing' converts to passive voice as 'is being written'.
questions.subject: English Grammar

questions.text: The Right of Children to Free and Compulsory Education (RTE) Act was passed by the Indian Parliament in the year:
questions.explanation: The RTE Act was enacted in 2009 and came into force on 1 April 2010, guaranteeing free and compulsory education to children aged 6–14 years.
questions.subject: Education Policy

questions.text: Which of the following is the best example of a 'formative' assessment?
options: The final board examination held at year-end
A short classroom quiz used to check understanding during a lesson
A one-time entrance test for school admission
questions.explanation: Formative assessment happens during the learning process to monitor progress and guide teaching, unlike summative assessment (like final exams), which evaluates learning after it is complete.
questions.subject: Child Development & Pedagogy

tests.title: UPSC Civil Services Prelims (GS Paper 1) Practice Set
tests.description: Polity, history, geography, economy, and current-affairs based general studies practice in the pattern of the UPSC Civil Services Preliminary Examination.

questions.text: Which Article of the Indian Constitution empowers citizens to move the Supreme Court directly for the enforcement of Fundamental Rights?
questions.explanation: Article 32 provides the Right to Constitutional Remedies, which Dr. B.R. Ambedkar called the 'heart and soul' of the Constitution.
questions.subject: Indian Polity

questions.text: The Tropic of Cancer does NOT pass through which of the following Indian states?
questions.explanation: The Tropic of Cancer passes through 8 Indian states: Gujarat, Rajasthan, Madhya Pradesh, Chhattisgarh, Jharkhand, West Bengal, Tripura, and Mizoram. Punjab is not among them.
questions.subject: Indian Geography

questions.text: The Permanent Settlement of Bengal (1793) was introduced by:
questions.explanation: Lord Cornwallis introduced the Permanent Settlement in 1793, fixing land revenue permanently and creating the zamindari system in Bengal.
questions.subject: Modern Indian History

questions.text: Which organization is the primary regulator of the insurance sector in India?
questions.explanation: The Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India (IRDAI) regulates and promotes the insurance industry in India.
questions.subject: Indian Economy

questions.text: India's Green Revolution, led by high-yield seed varieties, is most strongly associated with a dramatic rise in the production of which crop?
questions.explanation: The Green Revolution of the 1960s-70s, driven by high-yielding wheat varieties and championed by M.S. Swaminathan, primarily boosted wheat output, especially in Punjab and Haryana.
questions.subject: Indian Economy

questions.text: As per Article 80 of the Constitution, what is the maximum number of members the President can nominate to the Rajya Sabha?
questions.explanation: Under Article 80, the President may nominate up to 12 members to the Rajya Sabha for their expertise in art, literature, science, and social service.
questions.subject: Indian Polity

questions.text: Which of the following are Fundamental Duties listed under Article 51A of the Indian Constitution? (Select all that apply)
options: To abide by the Constitution and respect its ideals
To protect and improve the natural environment
To pay income tax honestly and on time
To develop scientific temper and the spirit of inquiry
questions.explanation: Article 51A lists duties such as respecting the Constitution, protecting the environment, and developing scientific temper. Paying income tax is a legal obligation, not a listed Fundamental Duty.
questions.subject: Indian Polity

questions.text: Who was the first Indian to be awarded a Nobel Prize?
questions.explanation: Rabindranath Tagore won the Nobel Prize in Literature in 1913 for 'Gitanjali', becoming the first Indian and first Asian Nobel laureate.
questions.subject: Static GK

questions.text: Chilika Lake, India's largest brackish-water lagoon, is located in which state?
questions.explanation: Chilika Lake, a Ramsar wetland site known for migratory birds and Irrawaddy dolphins, is located in Odisha.
questions.subject: Indian Geography

questions.text: The Fifth Five-Year Plan (1974-79) is best known for adopting which key objective as its central slogan?
questions.explanation: The Fifth Five-Year Plan adopted the objective of 'Garibi Hatao' (removal of poverty) alongside the goal of attaining self-reliance.
questions.subject: Indian Economy

tests.title: JEE Main Full Syllabus Practice Set (Physics, Chemistry & Maths)
tests.description: Combined Physics, Chemistry, and Mathematics practice questions covering the full JEE Main syllabus at exam-level difficulty.

questions.text: A particle moving in a straight line with uniform acceleration covers 10 m in the 1st second and 25 m in the 3rd second. What is its acceleration (in m/s²)?
questions.explanation: Distance in the nth second: s_n = u + a(n - 0.5). So s1 = u + 0.5a = 10 and s3 = u + 2.5a = 25. Subtracting gives 2a = 15, so a = 7.5 m/s².
questions.subject: Physics - Kinematics

questions.text: The dimensional formula of Planck's constant (h) is the same as that of:
questions.explanation: Planck's constant has SI units J·s = kg·m²/s, which is identical to the dimensional formula of angular momentum.
questions.subject: Physics - Units & Dimensions

questions.text: Which of the following period-3 elements has the highest first ionization energy?
questions.explanation: First ionization energies (kJ/mol): Na≈496, Mg≈738, Al≈577, Si≈786. Al is lower than Mg due to its stable filled 3s² configuration, but overall Si has the highest value among these four.
questions.subject: Chemistry - Periodic Properties

questions.text: Which gas is produced when zinc reacts with dilute hydrochloric acid?
questions.explanation: Zn + 2HCl → ZnCl₂ + H₂↑. Zinc displaces hydrogen gas from dilute hydrochloric acid.
questions.subject: Chemistry - Reactions

questions.text: What is the pH of a 0.001 M HCl solution, assuming complete dissociation?
questions.subject: Chemistry - Ionic Equilibrium

questions.text: What is the derivative of sin(x)·cos(x) with respect to x?
questions.explanation: d/dx[sin x · cos x] = cos²x - sin²x = cos(2x), using the product rule and double-angle identity.
questions.subject: Maths - Calculus

questions.text: If α and β are the roots of x² - 7x + 12 = 0, what is the value of α + β?
questions.explanation: For a quadratic ax² + bx + c = 0, the sum of roots = -b/a = -(-7)/1 = 7.
questions.subject: Maths - Quadratic Equations

questions.text: How many distinct arrangements can be made using all the letters of the word 'LEVEL'?
questions.explanation: LEVEL has 5 letters with L repeated twice and E repeated twice: 5!/(2!×2!) = 120/4 = 30.
questions.subject: Maths - Permutations & Combinations

questions.text: Which of the following statements about Simple Harmonic Motion (SHM) are correct? (Select all that apply)
options: Acceleration is proportional to displacement and directed towards the mean position
For a simple pendulum with small oscillations, the time period is independent of amplitude
Maximum velocity occurs at the extreme positions
Maximum acceleration occurs at the mean position
questions.explanation: In SHM, acceleration ∝ -displacement (restoring force towards mean position), and period is amplitude-independent for small oscillations. However, velocity is maximum at the mean position (not extremes) and acceleration is maximum at the extremes (not the mean), so statements 3 and 4 are false.
questions.subject: Physics - Oscillations

questions.text: Which of the following is an example of a covalent network (giant covalent) solid?
questions.explanation: Diamond is made of carbon atoms covalently bonded in a continuous 3D lattice, making it a covalent network solid — unlike NaCl (ionic), ice (molecular, H-bonded) or iron (metallic).
questions.subject: Chemistry - Bonding & Solid State

tests.title: NEET UG Full Syllabus Practice Set (Biology, Physics & Chemistry)
tests.description: Combined Biology, Physics, and Chemistry practice questions covering the full NEET UG syllabus at exam-level difficulty.

questions.text: Which cell organelle is known as the 'powerhouse of the cell'?
questions.explanation: Mitochondria generate ATP through cellular respiration, earning them the nickname 'powerhouse of the cell'.
questions.subject: Biology - Cell Biology

questions.text: The functional and structural unit of the kidney is called the:
questions.explanation: The nephron is the basic functional unit of the kidney, responsible for filtering blood and forming urine.
questions.subject: Biology - Human Physiology

questions.text: Which of the following are functions of the human liver? (Select all that apply)
options: Production of bile for fat digestion
questions.explanation: The liver detoxifies substances, produces bile, and stores glycogen. Insulin is produced by the beta cells of the pancreas, not the liver.
questions.subject: Biology - Human Physiology

questions.text: DNA replication is described as 'semi-conservative' because:
options: Both resulting DNA strands are entirely new
Each daughter DNA molecule has one parental strand and one newly synthesized strand
Only one strand out of two is ever replicated
questions.explanation: The Meselson-Stahl experiment demonstrated that DNA replication is semi-conservative — each new DNA molecule retains one original (parental) strand and one newly synthesized strand.
questions.subject: Biology - Genetics

questions.text: A convex lens of focal length 20 cm has an object placed 30 cm in front of it. At what distance (in cm) from the lens is the image formed?
questions.explanation: Using the lens formula 1/v - 1/u = 1/f with u = -30 cm, f = 20 cm: 1/v = 1/20 - 1/30 = 1/60, so v = 60 cm.
questions.subject: Physics - Optics

questions.text: The SI unit of electrical resistance is:
questions.explanation: Electrical resistance is measured in Ohms (Ω), as defined by Ohm's Law (V = IR).
questions.subject: Physics - Electricity

questions.text: What is the correct IUPAC name for CH₃-CH₂-OH?
questions.explanation: CH₃-CH₂-OH is a two-carbon alcohol, correctly named Ethanol under IUPAC nomenclature.
questions.subject: Chemistry - Organic Chemistry

questions.text: How many moles of oxygen (O₂) are needed to completely combust 1 mole of methane (CH₄)?
questions.explanation: The balanced combustion equation is CH₄ + 2O₂ → CO₂ + 2H₂O, so 2 moles of O₂ are required per mole of methane.
questions.subject: Chemistry - Stoichiometry

questions.text: Photosynthesis primarily occurs in which part of a plant cell?
questions.explanation: Chloroplasts contain chlorophyll and are the site of photosynthesis in plant cells.
questions.subject: Biology - Plant Physiology

questions.text: Which blood group is commonly known as the 'universal donor'?
questions.explanation: O negative blood lacks A, B, and Rh antigens, making it compatible with the widest range of recipients in emergency transfusions.
questions.subject: Biology - Human Physiology
